observations: The renderer exposed neither global require nor global process.
The preload exposed exactly seventeen named domain/shell members and no raw IPC primitive, Harness MessagePort, path, generic command method, or Skill invocation method.
IPC sender validation accepted only the expected main frame and webContents identity.
BrowserWindow reported contextIsolation=true, sandbox=true, nodeIntegration=false, webSecurity=true, and webviewTag=false.
The application menu contained native macOS Services, Edit, Window, and Clark view accelerators.
Keyboard navigation opened Focus, Canvas, Memory, and Connections; Canvas arrow navigation moved semantic focus across the live Idea Foundry graph.
Only one visible level-one heading was present per view, with named tabs, lists, buttons, panels, and live status semantics.

A real idea run reached exact-version approval, survived renderer reload, then created revision 2; the superseded run became cancelled, its approval became invalidated, and only the new exact brief completed after creator approval.

The Canvas projected idea, governed MCP assessment, five open evidence gates, deterministic brief, and exact-version review with root/parent lineage, structural readiness, and no false validation claim.
Visual QA regenerated live Focus, Canvas, governed Memory, Tool Pack, quarantined Skill, and promoted Skill captures; Memory has both top-of-workspace and decision-detail evidence.
The child-channel guard rejected unknown/mismatched response IDs and replayed/reordered events; diagnostics redacted credential-like material.

The supervised Harness received an allowlisted environment without ambient credentials, recovered one run after forced process death, and returned to the approval gate without duplicate artifact mutation.

Electron exercised proposal-only memory from an exact brief/run, verified pre-promotion retrieval exclusion, promoted and retrieved the claim, created an append-only correction with the old claim disputed, promoted the replacement, and forgot it with active-projection redaction.

The Memory workspace exposed layer, confidence, sensitivity, retrieval policy, exact evidence, scope, state, correction lineage actions, destination-aware retrieval, explicit-only authorization, and the current immutable-log deletion limitation.

Connections rendered the event-backed OpenCut Tool Pack identity, immutable source and manifest hashes, preferred integration path, component counts, and all eleven activation/rollback gates instead of a static marketing card.

The Tool Pack inspector showed immutable source integrity passing while the unsupported interface, legal, supply-chain, acquisition, adapter, capability, activation, and rollback evidence remained incomplete; activation stayed visibly disabled and OpenCut contributed zero executable capabilities.
The narrow resolveToolPackage preload command requires exact workspace, package, revision, action, and reason, while the Harness remains the sole authority for activation and rollback decisions.

Connections rendered the real bundled Class A Skill source hash, package file/executable counts, requested and trusted ceilings, test status, and all eleven promotion gates from the replayable Harness projection.

Electron promoted the exact quarantined Skill revision through the narrow resolveSkill command, then showed two revision trust scopes while explicitly stating that no Skill code executed, no run approval was inferred, and no direct invocation endpoint exists.

The narrow evaluateSkill command reread exact package bytes when the creator selected Recheck skill; the renderer had no invokeSkill API, and Skill promotion remained a reversible trust decision rather than a hidden execution path.

packagedExecution.observation: The unpacked macOS application launched its Harness utility entry from the ASAR, entered SQLite WAL mode, ran the governed MCP thesis assessment to durable approval, loaded the exact blocked OpenCut Tool Pack manifest with eleven gates and zero OpenCut capabilities, verified and quarantined the real bundled Class A Skill bytes with eleven passing gates and zero trusted scopes, and returned evidence-honest content-addressed state through the narrow preload API.

localBundleInspection.interpretation: The bundle is suitable only for local interaction and process inspection. It is not a signed, hardened, notarized, stapled, or Gatekeeper-accepted release artifact.

unimplementedNativeFlows: Developer ID release signing and notarization
safe third-party Tool Pack acquisition/unpack/build and a real active external Tool Pack
community Skill acquisition/signatures/advisories, production Class B/C execution, and run-scoped Skill invocation receipts
general third-party MCP, model, reflection/semantic-memory, social, and publishing flows
cryptographic memory erasure or event-log compaction
